title: A small solar lamp at home
summary: Describe a simple solar lamp and explain how this small piece of clean technology changed one part of daily life.
topic: A small solar lamp or solar charger used at home
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about a small solar lamp, light, or charger in your home or your family's home. Say where it sits, how it works in simple words, and what you can do now that you could not do before. Use present simple, past simple, and 'and', 'but', 'because'.
hintStarters: We have a small solar lamp on…
In the day it sits in the sun and at night…
Before the lamp, we used… but now…
sampleText: We have a small solar lamp on the windowsill in our kitchen. It is not big, but it is very useful. In the day it sits in the sun and the sun charges its battery. We do not pay anything because the energy is free. At night I switch it on and the light is soft and warm. My little sister reads her books under it before she sleeps. Before the lamp, we used candles when the power went off. Candles are not safe and the light is weak. One night last year the power went off for three hours. The solar lamp gave us light the whole time. My mother was very happy and we did not need any matches. Now the power cuts do not scare us. The lamp is small, but it makes a big difference. It saves a little money on our electricity bill too. I like clean energy because it is quiet and it does not make smoke. I think every home should have one small solar light.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mặt trời sạc cho pin của nó
nó tạo ra sự khác biệt lớn
tiết kiệm được một ít tiền

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for how things work, past simple for one night
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn để giải thích cách một vật hoạt động ('the sun charges its battery', 'I switch it on') và quá khứ đơn để kể một lần cụ thể ('the power went off', 'my mother was very happy'). Đừng trộn lẫn hai thì.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple to explain how something works ('the sun charges its battery', 'I switch it on') and the past simple for one specific time ('the power went off', 'my mother was very happy'). Keep the tenses separate.

sampleTranslationVi: Chúng tôi có một chiếc đèn năng lượng mặt trời nhỏ trên bệ cửa sổ trong bếp. Nó không to, nhưng rất hữu ích. Ban ngày nó nằm dưới nắng và mặt trời sạc cho pin của nó. Chúng tôi không phải trả tiền gì vì năng lượng là miễn phí. Ban đêm tôi bật nó lên và ánh sáng dịu và ấm. Em gái nhỏ của tôi đọc sách dưới đèn trước khi đi ngủ. Trước khi có đèn, chúng tôi dùng nến khi mất điện. Nến không an toàn và ánh sáng yếu. Một đêm năm ngoái điện bị cúp suốt ba tiếng. Chiếc đèn mặt trời cho chúng tôi ánh sáng suốt thời gian đó. Mẹ tôi rất vui và chúng tôi không cần đến diêm. Bây giờ những lần cúp điện không làm chúng tôi sợ. Chiếc đèn nhỏ, nhưng nó tạo ra sự khác biệt lớn. Nó cũng tiết kiệm được một ít tiền trên hóa đơn tiền điện. Tôi thích năng lượng sạch vì nó yên tĩnh và không tạo ra khói. Tôi nghĩ mỗi ngôi nhà nên có một chiếc đèn mặt trời nhỏ.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'The sun ___ its battery.'
options: charges
charge
charging
charged
mcQuestions.hint: The subject is 'the sun' — what ending does the verb need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best explains why you pay nothing?
options: We do not pay anything because energy free.
We do not pay anything because the energy is free.
We do not pay anything so the free energy.
Because free energy we do not anything pay.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' is followed by a full clause: 'the energy is free'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple for one night last year?
options: The power goed off for three hours.
The power go off for three hours.
The power went off for three hours.
The power is going off for three hours last year.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'went' is the irregular past simple of 'go'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' correctly to show a contrast?
options: The lamp is small, but big difference it makes.
The lamp small is, but it makes difference big.
But the lamp is small it makes a big difference.
The lamp is small, but it makes a big difference.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two full clauses; the second has subject + verb + object.
mcQuestions.hint: Both parts joined by 'but' need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'I ___ it on at night.'
options: switch
switches
switching
switched
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'I' the present simple takes no -s: 'switch'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural description of the light?
options: The light is softly and warmly.
The light is soft and warm.
The light are soft and warm.
The light is soft and warm and loud and big.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'is soft and warm' uses two adjectives correctly after 'is'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple about the candles?
options: Before the lamp, we use candles.
Before the lamp, we used to candles.
Before the lamp, we used candles.
Before the lamp, we are used candles.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'used' is the correct past simple of 'use'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option is a clean past-simple verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds the most natural about saving money?
options: It save a little money on our electricity bill.
It saves a little money on our electricity bill machine.
It is save a little money on our electricity bill.
It saves a little money on our electricity bill.
mcQuestions.hint: The subject 'it' needs which verb ending?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best explains why you like clean energy?
options: I like clean energy because it is quiet and it does not make smoke.
I like clean energy because quiet and no smoke.
I like clean energy so it is quiet smoke.
Because quiet and no smoke I clean energy like.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' is followed by two full clauses joined with 'and'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural way to give your opinion at the end?
options: I think every home should has one small solar light.
I think every home should have one small solar light.
I think every home should to have one small solar light.
I think every homes should have one small solar light.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'should have' (modal + base verb) with singular 'every home'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'should', use the base verb, and check 'every home' is singular.

criticalThinkingQuestion: What is one small piece of technology in your home that you would miss the most if it stopped working, and why?
